rationale: The citing paper uses Van Bruggen, Lilien, and Kacker (2002) in method-critical sections to justify aggregating responses from multiple experts to improve reliability of stated competitive-reaction data. This citation appears in both the respondent-selection discussion and the enrichment procedure where pooled expert inputs are converted into scenario-level allocations. Because this guidance directly informs how the authors construct and validate their core stated-data component, the role is more than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing study applies the cited paper's key-informant aggregation logic to its expert-elicitation design, explicitly using multiple respondents and pooled responses to increase data reliability. This supports the operationalization of the stated competitive-reaction inputs in the new data-enrichment method.
